sections.paragraph:
constr. מְאַת f.-

(1) a hundred, Arab. مِأَيةُ according to Kam., commonly مِيءَةُ [“Eth. ምአት፡, Syr. ܡܳܐܐ”]. It is prefixed to substantives in absol. and constr. state, מֵאָה שָׁנָה Gen 17:17, and מְאַת שָׁנָה 25:7 it is more rarely put after them, and this is only in the later books; רִמֹּנִים מֵאָה 2Ch 3:16. Dual מָאתַיִם (contr. for מְאָתַיִם) two hundred, Gen 11:23. Pl. מֵאֹות-(a) hundreds more than one; hence with other numerals שֵׁשׁ מֵאֹות “six hundred,” Exo 12:37 שְׁמֹנֶה מֵאֹות “eight hundred,” Gen 5:7, etc. Once-(b) one hundred, as if centena, 2 Ch. 25:9 כתיב (compare verse ). A less common form is מאיות (read מְאָיֹות), 2Ki 11:4, 2Ki 11:9, 10 2Ki 11:10, 15 2Ki 11:15 כתיב, compare Arab. مِأَيةُ.

(2) adv. a hundred times, Pro 17:10 constr. מְאַת id. Ecc 8:12.

(3) the hundredth part, i.e. of money or any thing, paid monthly, as being exacted in usury, Neh 5:11, מְאַת הַבֶּסֶף וְהַדָּגָן. Vulg. centesima. As to the centesima of the Romans, see Ernesti Cl. Cic. h. v.; as to the heavy usury still customary in the East, see Volney’s Travels, vol. ii. p. 410.

(4) [Meah], pr.n. of a tower at Jerusalem, Neh 3:1 12:39 .
